decisions: Retained all daily logs (04-21 through 04-24): oldest (04-21) is 4 days old, within 7-day window. memory/daily/2026-04-21.md contains 1 bootstrap entry, 04-22 has 2 entries, 04-23 has 3 entries, 04-24 has 4 entries — all heartbeat/consolidation only.

Skipped tier promotion for all 4 tiers: reviewed content of all 4 daily logs and found zero user-generated content. Every entry documents heartbeat consolidation runs. Promoting maintenance metadata to semantic/core would add noise without value.
observations: Brain has been idle for 5 days since bootstrap — all memory tiers (core, semantic, episodic, procedural) remain empty except for 2 episodic reflections documenting the idle state itself.
Daily log volume is growing from repetitive consolidation entries (4 runs on 04-24 alone) but contains no actionable content to promote.
The maintenance process is running reliably but has had nothing substantive to process — the system works, it just has no input yet.
recommendations: On first real user session, prioritize populating core/PREFERENCES.md and core/LEARNINGS.md to establish a baseline for future consolidations.

Consider whether the consolidation frequency is appropriate for an idle brain — 4 runs in one day (04-24) produced no meaningful changes. The maintenance-guard.sh threshold may need adjustment at the platform level.
processImprovements: [self-critique] The consolidation process has been running repeatedly on an empty brain for 5 days. Each run produces nearly identical reports and changes. The process is technically correct but operationally wasteful — the guard script's 24h threshold doesn't account for brains with zero user activity.

[proposal] The maintenance-guard.sh could benefit from a secondary check: if the last N consolidation runs all reported 'no promotable content', extend the threshold (e.g., skip until a non-maintenance commit appears). This would reduce churn on idle brains.
[blocked] Cannot modify maintenance-guard.sh — it lives in base-brain which is read-only from channel brain context.
